title: Flaky Norwegian Salmon Skillet
description: A pungent and juicy Norwegian dish featuring hojicha, blanch, and FODMAP-friendly ingredients, cooked to perfection on a salt block vessel.
instructions: Preheat the salt block vessel to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together hojicha tea leaves, olive oil, salt, and black pepper to create a marinade.
Place the salmon fillets in the marinade and let it sit for 10 minutes.
Remove the salmon from the marinade and place it on the preheated salt block vessel.
Cook the salmon for 4 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the salmon is cooking, heat the garlic-infused oil in a small pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped parsley to the pan and cook until it is slightly wilted.
Serve the cooked salmon with the parsley sauce spooned over the top and a lemon wedge on the side.
